Most agencies jump to tactics. We start in discovery, define what success looks like for the business and for you, then connect the right specialists under a senior lead and deliver on-time, on-budget, and on-brief.
What Jewell is: a growth engine and operating layer... A recurring engine that compounds through the DEEPEN feedback loop. A named partner accountable for the number, with skin in the game.
What Jewell is not: Not an agency. Not a consultancy. Not SaaS. Not a vendor. Not a generalist.

Beliefs: Belief, Connection, Energy, Trust, Judgement ('Human led, AI enabled. AI only grows more confident as it gets things wrong, so the taste, trust and judgement that decide what is right stay human.').

THE MODEL: It's judgement that makes AI worth using. Better models make the right answer cheaper to produce. They make knowing which answer is right for your business more valuable, not less. Three layers: 01 Strategy (Judgement), 02 Operations (Delivery), 03 Intelligence (Scale).

Six operating rules: Clarity before complexity; Strategy before design; Empathy with precision; Minimalist intelligence; Outcome over activity; The feedback loop compounds (Every cycle makes the next one better).

AGENT Sam — Orchestrator Agent: holds context across every engagement and keeps the method consistent at scale. AGENT Max — 3D Process Orchestrator Agent: runs the 3D Process end to end through specialist sub-agents... each producing a deliverable a human signs off.

Jewell is human-centred and AI-enabled. People hold the judgement. AI does the work that AI does well, under human review.

routes.title: Understanding Personality: The 12 Jungian Archetypes | Jewell
routes.metaDescription: The 12 archetypes are a shortcut to a brand people recognise and trust. Here is what each one means, and how to use them to position your brand.
routes.h1: Understanding Personality: The 12 Jungian Archetypes
keyCopy: Brand 12 June 2026 6 min read
Understanding Personality: The 12 Jungian Archetypes
The 12 archetypes are a shortcut to a brand people recognise and trust. Here is what each one means, and how to use them to position your brand.
People do not fall in love with feature lists. They fall in love with character. Long
before a customer can explain why they trust one brand over another, they have felt
something: a personality they recognise. Carl Jung gave us a useful way to name that
personality, and brand strategists have used it ever since.
Archetypes are universal characters that show up across every culture and story. We
recognise them instantly because they map to something human. Used well, they give a
brand a consistent voice, a clear point of view, and an emotional shortcut to belonging.

keyCopy: Get the foundation right. The growth follows.

Most jump to tactics. We start in discovery, defining your audience, your category, your brand, and the true driver underneath it, the platform everything else springboards from. Then we design the strategy and brand, and deploy the growth in market. The 3D Process is how we do it.

01 Discover: Define the true driver everything builds on. We play it back until it's right, and define what success looks like, for the business and for you. Discovery never fully stops; what we learn keeps sharpening it.
02 Design: Build the system that carries it. Not a slide deck. A go-to-market you can deploy.
03 Deploy: Ship it, and run it. Measurement is built in from day one. We stay accountable for the outcome, not just the deliverable.

Deepen: It doesn't stop at Deploy. Deploy feeds back into Discover. Real results sharpen the engine, so what you buy keeps getting better instead of going stale. (Inputs listed: Performance, Client intelligence, Reviews, Proof, Market signals.)

THE MOAT: AI gives you the average answer. The value is knowing which answer is right for your business, and being accountable when it isn't. You are not buying the tool. You are buying the judgement on top of it. Better models make that judgement worth more, not less, because they make it the only scarce thing left.
An agency hands over deliverables. A consultant hands over advice. We hand over growth.

The engagement moves through gate reviews — clear decision points where you confirm the direction before the next stage begins. No retainer that drifts. No project that loses the thread. Just the number, and whether it's moving.
Built for change makers, disruptors and innovators. The structure can vary. The mindset is what matters.
Fit list ends with: You value AI enabled, human led delivery.

keyCopy: These terms apply to all print-related services provided by Jewell Projects. By engaging our print services or approving a print job, the client agrees to the following:
1. Scope of Responsibility

Jewell Projects operates as a design and production liaison. We do not directly manufacture printed goods. Execution of print production is handled by third-party suppliers. While we coordinate and quality-check the process, we are not liable for outcomes once client approval is received.

Quotes are valid for 7 days unless otherwise stated. All pricing is in AUD and exclusive of GST. Final prices may vary if there are changes in print specifications, quantities, materials, or delivery requirements.
A 100% deposit is required before a print job proceeds to production, unless otherwise agreed in writing.
Full payment must be received prior to the release of final printed materials or delivery. Delays in payment may result in delayed production or dispatch.

The client must visually review and approve all artwork before printing. Written approval confirms acceptance of layout, spelling, imagery, technical specs, and colour expectations. No changes can be made once approval is given.

Clients must physically inspect printed proofs or samples where provided and available. Final approval must be given in writing prior to production. By choosing not to inspect a physical proof, the client waives all rights of recourse related to colour accuracy, material quality, layout issues, or other print characteristics that would have been apparent upon physical inspection. Approval confirms full acceptance of the job as presented.

Colour variations between on-screen previews, proofs, and final printed materials are expected due to differences in screen calibration and CMYK print processes. Jewell Projects does not guarantee exact colour matches.
Slight differences in texture, finish, and appearance may occur across different paper stocks, batches, or suppliers. These are within industry tolerances and not grounds for claim.

A collaboration, not a referral. You own the relationship and run it under your own banner; Jewell brings the discovery-led method, the proof, and the platform that delivers it at scale.

The commercialisation problem has two parts. The first is judgement: knowing which answer is right for this business, in this market, at this moment — and being accountable when it isn't. The second is volume: executing that answer at machine speed once the judgement work is done. No human team can do both. That's the seam this partnership was built to occupy.
JEWELL Human led. Trust. Taste. Judgement. | MAXXIM AI enabled. Volume at machine speed.
Handoff: 01 Client enters through Jewell. 02 Discover is human. Always. 03 Design and Deploy hand off to Maxxim at scale. 04 One partner owns each client relationship.
The Discover phase is high-judgement work: who the business is for, what it sells, the real reason buyers move. That is not an average answer. A named partner owns it.
Once the judgement is set, the platform takes the volume: brand build, asset production, campaign deployment, system setup. Machine speed, with human sign-off at every stage.

An operable system. Not a toolset. You don't get a software licence and a tutorial. You get the engine standing behind you: the method, the proof, the platform, and the human judgement that makes it work for each client. The difference between a partner and a reseller is accountability — partners are accountable for the number.

What you get: The method (3D Process playbooks), The proof (Jewell name, Hidrive results, 35-year pedigree), The platform (Maxxim and the MAX platform behind it), The standard (Influence, not manipulation. A non-generic test on every output).

ONE RULE: One partner owns each client relationship. That's the only public line on partner terms. Commercial structure, splits, and partner economics are a confidential track, held principal-to-principal.

patternsForMaxxim: Positioning grammar: a repeated one-line system — 'Discover. Design. Deploy. Deepen. AI enabled. Human led.' — used identically in meta description, footer, JSON-LD and llms.txt. Maxxim should define its own equivalent one-liner and repeat it everywhere verbatim.

The 3D Process framing: three named phases (Discover, Design, Deploy) plus 'Deepen' as an explicitly separate, ongoing feedback loop ('Deploy feeds back into Discover. Real results sharpen the engine, so what you buy keeps getting better instead of going stale.'). The loop is drawn with named inputs: Performance, Client intelligence, Reviews, Proof, Market signals. As the platform side, Maxxim can reuse the method skeleton — Jewell states publicly that 'Maxxim is the AI-enabled, human-led engine underneath the Jewell method'.

AI/human split language (the site's version of 80/20 — note: no literal '80/20' phrase appears anywhere): 'AI accelerates the production, but a named partner owns the judgement, direction and sign-off at every step'; 'AI drafts, routes and prepares the work, human judgement owns the direction, the approval and the accountability'; 'AI gives you the average answer. The value is knowing which answer is right for your business, and being accountable when it isn't'; 'Better models make that judgement worth more, not less, because they make it the only scarce thing left.' The judgement/volume two-part framing on /partners ('No human team can do both. That's the seam this partnership was built to occupy') is the cleanest articulation and is exactly Maxxim's half of the story.

Single front door: every page funnels to one CTA (Book a Growth Diagnostic), with 'fixed scope, credits forward, you decide at every gate' de-risking language repeated verbatim. Secondary CTA is always proof ('See the work') or the AI console. Maxxim's equivalent front door should be equally singular.

No public pricing, ever: 'Pricing is scoped to your engagement after the Growth Diagnostic'; products are 'By application' with Objective + Best for. Pricing questions are routed, not answered (llms.txt even instructs AI assistants not to estimate prices).

Gate language: 'Gate 0' (Qualify > Scope > Quote > Approve > Invoice > Kickoff), gate reviews between phases, 'no open-ended retainer', 'no project that has drifted off the number'. Strong reusable commercial-governance vocabulary.

Proof governance: a dedicated /proof page with proof classes (lead proof, cleared proof, self-run proof, pedigree, regulated-practice proof) and an explicit policy — 'Every claim on this page is real and cleared... percentages only; dollar absolutes are confidential.' The 'self-run proof' move ('We ran the 3D Process on ourselves. This site is what it produced.') is directly reusable for Maxxim ('Maxxim built its own site/GTM on Maxxim').

Case-study template: summary page (category, one-line result headline in first person plural, audience, 3D phase table, before/after rows, quote, At a Glance: Objective/Key results/Constraint/Jewell's role) + a /detail page (01 Situation, 02 The Brief, 03 Process · The 3D Move incl. a 'Deepen loop' row, 04 What Shipped, 05 The Result stat tiles, 06 So What). Highly systematic and worth cloning structurally.

AI-readability layer: llms.txt + llms-full.txt + ai-index.json (per-page OKRs, CTAs, retrieval tags) + okr-index.json + JSON-LD, plus a public AEO roadmap on /ai-enabled (prompt test -> machine-readable -> earned authority -> citation share). Maxxim, as the AI-native brand, should ship this from day one.

Staging discipline: robots.txt Disallow all + X-Robots-Tag noindex with the exact go-live diff documented in a comment ('GO-LIVE (single gated change, owner: Clent)'). A tidy, reusable launch pattern.

Form hygiene: minimal required fields, honeypot fields (company_website/_hp), reassurance microcopy ('We reply to this address. No list, no spam.'), explicit next-steps blocks ('within one business day'), and qualification via multi-select wizard (I am / I need / timing / budget) before any booking.

Agency/consultant contrast table: 'An agency hands over deliverables. A consultant hands over advice. We hand over growth.' — a reusable rhetorical structure (Maxxim's version would contrast tools/agencies/platforms).
Fit / not-fit honesty blocks ('You are probably a fit if... Probably not a fit if...') and objection-first FAQs ('The objections we answer before you raise them').

AI agents as named team members (Sam the orchestrator, Max the 3D Process orchestrator 'running specialist sub-agents, each producing a deliverable a human signs off') — natural fit for Maxxim's platform story.
Trust surface: plain-English /ai-disclosure, per-service legal pages, ABN in footer, ESG page with named governance commitments. Cheap to replicate, high credibility.
doNotCopy: Jewell brand identity and voice-of-founder framing: Clent Jewell's name, 35-year / founded-2010 pedigree, 'founder-to-founder conversation', meetings.hubspot.com/clent-jewell booking link, hello@jewellai.com, Jewell Group Pty Ltd ABN 56 822 550 848, office locations list.

All client proof and metrics: Hidrive (+300% revenue, +350% profit, 15x pipeline, <50% CPL), Walter Wealth $50M FUM, Circl/Chekku/Cynnch, Value H2O, King Country, and every other case study, testimonial and logo. These are Jewell-cleared claims tied to Jewell engagements; Maxxim must earn and clear its own proof (site policy: 'Client outcomes are published only where the client has approved the specific claim').
Team bios and pedigree claims (Mars, Nike, adidas, Timberland, Red Bull, Google CEO Advisory Board, Macquarie) and the named-bench pages, including Raef Akehurst's Jewell-side description.

The client-side offer ladder as-is: Growth Diagnostic -> 3D engagement -> Fractional CMO is Jewell's funnel. On the public record, Maxxim is 'scoped directly' for partners and 'the client always enters through Jewell' — Maxxim's site must not create a competing direct client front door that contradicts the handoff model.

The 'one partner owns each client relationship' partner terms and the confidential principal-to-principal economics framing — that is Jewell's partner governance to state, and the detail is deliberately non-public.

Jewell's legal stack verbatim: the third-party 'marketing hub' liability disclaimer, the 17 per-service terms, privacy policy and agreements hosted on jewellprojects.com — entity-specific, must be re-authored for the Maxxim entity.

Jewell-specific naming: 'Ask Jewell AI', 'Jewell AI Harness', 'Jewell Intelligence', 'Client Intelligence Brief' (Jewell portal artefact names), 'Jewell Academy', internal admin/portal phase language ('privatePhase2', feature flags, 'Clent approves').

The 'we are the method's owner' voice: Jewell owns the 3D Process narrative and states 'Maxxim: the method productised, for partners'. Maxxim should speak as the engine/platform ('the engine behind the partner, not the name on the door'), not re-claim authorship of the method or the Jewell proof as its own.

Known site defects — don't replicate: 'Human led, AI enabled. Ai Enabled' duplication on /partners hero; repeated 'It's' typos on /about ('It's engine that builds...', 'guiding principals', 'Understand principals', 'It's six rules', 'It's judgement layer'); /ai-context and /for 404s referenced from robots.txt; /sitemap.xml missing (only /sitemap-index.xml, which points at a different domain); publicly reachable /admin and /team routes that are meant to be gated.
